rationale: Analysis of 28 AI Infrastructure Engineer postings from 2026-03 to 2026-05 confirms the existing 10 modules already cover the universal table-stakes (Python, Linux, Docker, Kubernetes, cloud, Terraform, CI/CD, observability, GPU, MLOps, data pipelines, LLM serving with vLLM/Triton, RAG, vector DBs, fine-tuning, cost optimization). The single material shift in the last 90 days that the mid-level (L20) AI Infrastructure Engineer role genuinely owns is the rise of *AI agent infrastructure* — production platforms that host, orchestrate, observe, and govern multi-agent and MCP-based systems. Capstone explicitly lists Model Context Protocol implementations; Yuno's posting is literally titled 'AI Agent Infrastructure'; Autohive's product is multi-agent; Scale AI's enterprise platform calls out 'secure, scalable Agentic workflows.' This is L20 work because it sits on top of the K8s/cloud/observability stack the engineer already owns — not deep performance tuning (L35), not multi-cloud architecture (L30+), not leadership (L40+). Other emerging trends — LLM-D / disaggregated serving (L35 Performance), Slurm+K8s hybrid orchestration (L30 ML Platform), InfiniBand/RDMA tuning (L35 Performance) — are linked from JOB_REQUIREMENTS.md to their natural higher-level owners rather than duplicated here. No additions are proposed for those.

modules.title: AI Agent Infrastructure
modules.description: Build the production infrastructure underneath agentic LLM applications: hosting Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers, orchestrating multi-agent workflows, securing tool calls, observing agent traces (token cost, tool latency, hallucination signals), running multi-tenant agent platforms with per-tenant quotas, and managing agent lifecycle (versioning, rollout, kill switches, sandboxing). Builds directly on Module 10 (LLM Infrastructure) and Module 04 (Kubernetes). Out of scope: writing the agents themselves — this is the platform underneath them.
modules.rationale: Mid-level AI Infrastructure Engineer postings in March-May 2026 increasingly require production experience with AI agent platforms and MCP, but the existing curriculum (Module 10) stops at single-model LLM serving + RAG. The gap is structural, not a single missing exercise: hosting MCP servers, securing tool calls, multi-agent orchestration, agent-trace observability, and agent lifecycle management together form a distinct production discipline that warrants its own module. The L20 role owns this because it sits on the same K8s/cloud/observability stack the engineer already runs (per JOB_REQUIREMENTS.md sections 2-4 and 7) — it is not deep performance tuning (L35) or multi-cloud architecture (L30+) or research engineering. Recommended exercise scaffolding for human reviewers to populate: (a) host an MCP server on Kubernetes with secrets, RBAC, and rate limiting, (b) build an agent observability stack (token cost + tool-call latency + replay) with Langfuse or OpenTelemetry, (c) deploy a multi-tenant agent platform with per-tenant quotas and sandboxed tool execution, (d) implement a kill switch and canary rollout for an agent in production. Covers JOB_REQUIREMENTS.md section 7 (AI agent infrastructure).

evidence.title: AI Infrastructure Engineer
evidence.quote: Hands-on work with AI agent frameworks, LLM integrations, or Model Context Protocol (MCP) implementations... Design and implement orchestration frameworks for LLM-powered tools and agent workflows... Build Model Context Protocol services for enterprise system integration

evidence.title: Staff Infrastructure Software Engineer, Enterprise AI
evidence.quote: Define architectural patterns for secure, scalable Agentic workflows... Hands-on experience and a passion for working with Agents, LLMs, vector databases, and other emerging AI technologies

evidence.title: Senior Platform Engineer — AI Agent Infrastructure
evidence.quote: Yuno is building a platform that provisions, deploys, and manages AI agents at scale on AWS, requiring someone to own infrastructure and ensure the system is reliable, observable, and ready to scale

evidence.title: AI Infrastructure Engineer
evidence.quote: Autohive is building the future of AI-driven work automation, enabling users to discover, build, and deploy AI agents that work together to eliminate toil and maximize productivity
